# U3: `mtt add --json` emits the created task object (not the plain `created t1`),
# so an agent gets the fresh id without parsing prose (`... --json | jq -r .id`).

exec mtt init

# The default type (task) is a root, so the first add just works.
exec mtt --json add 'first task'
stdout '"id": "t1"'
stdout '"status": "tbd"'
! stdout 'created t1'

# Without --json, the plain ack is unchanged.
exec mtt add 'second task'
stdout 'created t2'
